key_predictive_features.description: G/S ratio currently ~57:1, compressed from 100:1 in April 2025. Near long-term mean of 60-70:1. Silver surged 147% in 2025 vs gold's 67%. The dramatic mean-reversion trade has largely played out. At 57:1, ratio is slightly below mean — further compression possible but upside is diminishing. ML-gated mean reversion achieves Sharpe 0.68-0.73.
key_predictive_features.our_status: metals_cross_assets.py tracks ratio velocity but not z-score. At current 57:1, the z-score would be near 0 or slightly negative relative to 50-year mean, meaning no strong directional signal from ratio alone. Still worth implementing for future extremes.

key_predictive_features.feature: Silver Price vs All-Time High Distance
key_predictive_features.description: Current ~$79-80, ATH was $121.67 (Jan 29, 2026). Price is ~35% below ATH. The pullback from $121 to $79 is a -35% drawdown — historically, silver drawdowns of 25-40% from cycle peaks have been followed by secondary rallies in 60% of cases within 3-6 months.
key_predictive_features.our_status: Not explicitly tracked. Could add ATH-distance as a mean-reversion feature in the structure signal. Simple to implement.

key_predictive_features.description: PV Magazine reported Apr 15 that silver demand from PV industry expected to DROP 19% in 2026 to ~194M oz, despite growing installations. Manufacturers cutting silver per-cell usage due to costs. This is a bearish fundamental revision that contradicts the pure bull narrative.
key_predictive_features.our_status: Not tracked in real-time. This is a material fundamental shift — our system's Layer 2 macro thesis (silver_bull prophecy, 0.8 conviction) should be re-evaluated. The industrial demand pillar is weaker than assumed.

key_predictive_features.description: Silver maintains -0.55 to -0.70 correlation with DXY. Dollar direction remains the single strongest macro driver for silver. US-Iran peace talks easing geopolitical premium, which is dollar-positive (bearish silver). Need to watch Fed rate path closely.
key_predictive_features.our_status: Partially captured by macro_regime (30.3% accuracy). DXY component should be isolated as standalone sub-signal. The weak macro_regime accuracy is likely diluted by non-predictive sub-components.

key_predictive_features.description: April has 80% positive occurrence rate (2020-2024), but we're entering the May-June seasonal weakness window. Historical pattern: strong Jan-Apr rally followed by correction into June. Silver is currently at the tail end of the bullish seasonal window. Q1 seasonal rally (Jan 15 - Apr 09) already ended.

key_predictive_features.description: WGC forecasts ~850 tonnes of gold purchases by central banks in 2026 (down from 1000+ in 2022-2024 but still elevated). China and Kazakhstan active buyers. This provides a structural floor for gold, which indirectly supports silver through G/S ratio. However, the reduction from peak buying pace is a mild headwind.
key_predictive_features.our_status: Not directly tracked. Gold's structural bid from central banks is captured indirectly through XAU-USD price. No action needed — this is background context for Layer 2.

COMEX registered inventory vulnerable — only 124M oz registered, 26% withdrawn in one week in January

US-Iran peace talks reducing geopolitical premium — dollar-positive, silver-negative near-term
Silver 35% below ATH ($121.67 in Jan 2026) — historical precedent for 60% probability of secondary rally within 3-6 months
India physical demand remains strong for industrial and investment use

XAG at $79-80 is in no-man's-land: 35% below ATH but no clear catalyst for re-acceleration. HOLD bias for new positions, trail stops on existing.

key_predictive_features.description: Current reading 1.089 — moderate profit-taking without euphoria. Values above 1.0 mean coins moved at a profit. The 1.089 reading is healthy — not overheated (>1.15) and not capitulation (<1.0). Consistent with range-bound consolidation.

Should add persistence tracking: consecutive periods of extreme negative funding. 3+ consecutive = very high confidence.

key_predictive_features.description: Ratio reached 15x in early April — leverage-heavy futures dominating price discovery over organic spot demand. key_predictive_features.description: Six failed attempts to sustain above $76K in past two months. $75K is both round psychological number AND 100-day moving average. A daily close above $75K would trigger algorithmic buying. Below $70K is key support. The range is $70K-$76K with most activity clustered at $74K-$75K.
key_predictive_features.our_status: Structure signal (signal_structure.py) should capture this via breakout detection. But the repeated failure at $76K suggests our signal needs to weight failed breakout attempts as increasingly bearish until a decisive break occurs.

key_predictive_features.description: Highly volatile daily ETF flows: -$325.8M on Apr 13, +$411.4M on Apr 14, +$26M on Apr 16. Cumulative total $57.08B. YTD flows turned positive at $2.3B. BlackRock IBIT led with $871M. The daily volatility in ETF flows is creating noise in price action — net weekly flows are more predictive than daily.
key_predictive_features.our_status: Not directly tracked as a signal. ETF flow data available from Farside/CoinGlass. Should consider weekly net ETF flow as a sub-signal. Daily flows are too noisy.

Currently in post-peak consolidation phase. Power Law model projected peak $210K in Jan 2026 (missed), low $60K later in 2026. S2F model projects $65K-$524K range. Growing consensus that traditional 4-year cycle may be weakening due to institutional adoption.
key_predictive_features.our_status: Not explicitly modeled as a signal. The halving cycle position is useful context for Layer 2 but too low-frequency for signal system. Our prophecy framework (btc_range) captures this thesis qualitatively.

When perp/spot ratio exceeds 10x, market is leverage-dominated. Signal: elevated breakout probability, uncertain direction. Above 15x = extreme fragility. Use as volatility regime indicator to adjust confidence.

deep_dives.implementation_notes: BTC at $75K is in a high-tension equilibrium: deeply negative funding (historical bottom signal), persistent exchange outflows (accumulation), but six failed breakouts above $76K and leverage-dominated market (15x perp/spot). The most actionable edge is funding rate persistence — our best signal at 74.2% could be enhanced by tracking consecutive periods of extreme funding. The short squeeze setup ($200M above $75.5K) is real but timing is uncertain. For our signal system: the 3B/3S consensus split reflects genuine market uncertainty. RECOMMENDATION: lean bullish based on funding persistence and exchange outflows, but size conservatively until $76K is decisively broken. A daily close above $76K = full bullish, below $70K = reassess.

key_predictive_features.description: Currently 0.0313, bouncing from 2026 low of 0.028 in February. Must reclaim 0.035 on weekly close to signal genuine altcoin rotation (not just a squeeze). 0.040 would confirm trend change. ETH dominance at 10.4% — depressed. Historically, ETH/BTC ratio recovery from multi-year lows precedes strong ETH rallies.
key_predictive_features.our_status: Our eth_follows_btc prophecy is directionally correct. Need to add ETH/BTC ratio as an explicit feature. When ratio bounces from multi-year lows with volume, it's a high-conviction BUY signal for ETH specifically.

key_predictive_features.description: 35.8M ETH staked (~30% of supply), up from 11% in March 2023. 1.1M active validators. Staking yield 2.8-3.5%. The 30% staking lock is structurally bullish — reduces circulating supply. Staked ETH ETFs (BlackRock, Fidelity filings) could unlock new institutional demand. Post-Pectra max stake increased to 2,048 ETH per validator.
key_predictive_features.our_status: Not tracked as a signal feature. Staking percentage is a slow-moving structural indicator — useful for Layer 2 macro context. The rate of change in staking (new deposits vs withdrawals) would be more useful as a signal.

Mainnet gas at 0.052 Gwei (near zero). 284,000 new users in Q1 2026 (+82% QoQ). Stablecoin supply on ETH hit record $180B.

key_predictive_features.description: RSI for ETH dropped to 47.2% accuracy recently. Current RSI reading at 62.27 (neutral). The RSI degradation is likely because ETH has been range-bound in a mean-reverting pattern where RSI signals generate whipsaw. RSI works best in trending markets, poorly in ranging.
key_predictive_features.our_status: RSI accuracy gating should catch this — at 47.2% it's close to the 45% force-HOLD threshold. If it drops further, RSI will auto-disable for ETH. Consider: RSI accuracy may improve once ETH breaks out of its range. Regime-conditional RSI weighting would help.

Next upgrades: Glamsterdam (H1 2026) and Hegota (H2 2026) targeting parallel execution and 100M gas limits. PeerDAS upgrade expected to further scale L2 data availability.

key_predictive_features.our_status: This aligns with our signal engine output. The bear-leaning technical split suggests our signals are capturing the market's uncertainty correctly — the problem is not signal accuracy but market indecision.

papers_and_models.key_finding: L2s handle 95% of throughput. Base dominates with 46.6% TVL and 62% fee revenue. Only Base is profitable. Value accruing to Coinbase equity, not ETH. This is a structural challenge for ETH price.

recommended_new_signals.description: Track ETH/BTC ratio 7d rate of change. Rising ratio = rotation into ETH, falling = flight to BTC safety. Current bounce from 0.028 to 0.0313 is positive. Add as sub-signal in a crypto-rotation module.
Compare weekly net ETH ETF flows vs BTC ETF flows. When ETH flows positive while BTC negative, BUY ETH. When reversed, SELL/HOLD ETH. Captures institutional rotation dynamics.
Track net staking deposits/withdrawals per week. Rising net deposits = bullish (supply reduction). Withdrawals surge = bearish. Available from beaconcha.in API.

recommended_new_signals.description: Apply RSI only in trending regimes (ADX > 25 or price outside BB). In ranging regimes (current), suppress RSI weight to avoid whipsaw losses. This directly addresses the RSI accuracy drop to 47.2%.

deep_dives.implementation_notes: ETH's core problem is clear: the market is genuinely split (13 bullish vs 19 bearish indicators), and our signal system reflects this accurately with 3B/3S consensus. This isn't a signal accuracy problem — it's market indecision. The RSI accuracy drop to 47.2% is a symptom of ranging behavior, not a broken signal. FIX: regime-conditional RSI weighting (suppress in ranging, amplify in trending). CATALYST WATCH: (1) ETH/BTC ratio reclaiming 0.035 = genuine rotation, (2) staked ETH ETF approval = institutional catalyst, (3) Glamsterdam upgrade date confirmation. STRUCTURAL CONCERN: L2 value accrual paradox — network utility at ATH but fee revenue migrating to L2s (mainly Base/Coinbase), not to ETH holders. This challenges the long-term ETH value thesis. Near-term: lean cautiously bullish based on ETH/BTC ratio bounce and ETF flow divergence, but conviction is LOW (0.5) until $75K BTC breakout provides the rising-tide catalyst.
